titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Tom and Judy Dawson collection of playing card ephemera
(Dawson) Tom and Judy collection of playing card ephemera
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday May 26, 2021 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Tom and Judy Dawson Collection of Playing Card Ephemera

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains playing card-related material collected by Tom and Judy Dawson over 35 years. Its focus is on the playing card industry but also explores the popular culture of playing card imagery and its social impact. A highlight of the Dawsons' collection is early standard American and Canadian playing cards and ephemera related to the cards and their makers. Ephemera includes sample books, billheads, correspondence, advertising materials, trade cards, calendars, invitations, postcards and many other objects with playing card interest. Printed material in the collection includes books, booklets, and catalogs. The bulk of printed material are catalogs for card games and playing card culture, and booklets featuring playing card manufacturers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Tom and Judy Dawson on the Mary Flagler Cary Fund, 2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 33 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Tom and Judy Dawson Collection of Playing Card Ephemera is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tom and Judy Dawson Collection of Playing Card Ephemera. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Tom and Judy Dawson
bioghist.p: Tom and Judy Dawson are collectors of antiques and playing card ephemera. They have been collecting for over 40 years. Early on, the Dawsons were influenced by Gene Hochman, an avid collector and playing card enthusiast. Hochman was the author of the original Encyclopedia of American Playing Cards , which was re-written by the Dawsons in 2000. Before his retirement as a Certified Public Accountant, Tom was a senior partner at the accounting organization Deloitte. Judy pursues her interests in the areas of antique collecting, gardening, and home design, in addition to having raised their six children. In addition to caring for their collection, Tom and Judy serve as officers of 52 Plus Joker, a club for those interested in antique American and International playing cards. Judy also edits the club's quarterly publication, Clear the Decks .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Advertisements, 1812 - 1996. II. Ephemera, 1650 - 2009. III. Posters and artwork, 1835 - 1999. IV. Instruction and rule booklets and publications, 1861 - 2014. V. Objects, 1750 - 1938. VI. Computer media, undated.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Portions of description for this collection have been directly repurposed from description supplied with the collection upon its receipt. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Some material in the collection do not currently have assigned containers due to pending conservation review and housing.

did.unittitle: Advertisements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes advertisements featuring playing card imagery, as well as advertisements for various playing card brands and other games. The advertisements are in the form of booklets, fliers, magazine and newspaper advertisements, store cards, promotional cards, and trade cards. Material originates from Canada, England, France, Japan, and the United States.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by name of the advertiser.

Ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes ephemera featuring playing card imagery, as well as ephemera from playing card manufacturers. The ephemera consists of calendars, fliers, greeting cards, letterheads, menus, price lists, playing card sample books and folders, score cards, scorers, trump indicators, valentines, and wrappers. Material originates from Belgium, Canada, Denmark, England, France, Germany, and the United States.

Playing card samples, sample folders, and sample books

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by name of playing card deck.

Postcards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards depicting playing cards, playing card imagery, and postcards from playing card manufacturers. Material originates from England, France, Germany, Italy, and the United States.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by playing card manufacturer or catalog name.

Score cards, scorers, and trump indicators

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes whist markers, score cards, scorers, and trump indicators. Material is made of brass, celluloid, paper, mother of pearl, and metal.

did.unittitle: Five English sliding whist scorers: three de la Rue and two Goodall
Four French enameled whist markers featuring kings on one side and a double, triple, and quadruple on the other side
Four French enameled whist markers featuring kings on one side and a double, triple, and quadruple on the other side housed in decorative box
Handwritten score card for the Game of Boston
English Cavendish Bezique paper indicator
Two brass whist markers with registry marks
Two American Hyatt's paper trump indicators
English brass clicking scorer with two wheels and bakelite base
Four unlabelled celluloid and paper trump indicators
Four mother of pearl scorers featuring carved designs and beadwork depicting three cards
Four mother of pearl scorers with carved designs in small leather box
English scorer for the game of Bezique
Six finely carved whist scorers in a container featuring ivory open carved details, four show suit signs, and entwined initials
Two French whist scorers
Three punched tally cards
English Tom Thumb wooden whist marker
Four ivory scorers in carved ivory box with detailed carvings and layered petals
Ivory box with detailed carvings with four serrated scorers
Ivory box with four serrated scorers
Two whist ivory scorers with carved hand rotating pointers
Whist score card: hand holding spade flush, advertisement for Wright's Coal Tar Soap
Heart-shaped Evanola Manf. Euchre score card

Four carved ivory whist scorers with open cut work on box and scorers
Four ivory whist scorers in ivory circular case with text "Whist"
Four mother of pearl scorers with hinged brass box featuring an embossed female head
Double silver plated card scorer with two rotating celluloid wheels showing the value through heart and spade pips
Two trump indicators and four-sided bridge scorer for The Molassine Co. Ltd.
Clarendon R E Weston whist game counter housed in box
American score card for 500 featuring a singing woman
American celluloid trump indicator for Aetna Life
American scoring card featuring King of Clubs

American celluloid trump indicator and scorer for Ditzler Color Co.
American celluloid trump indicator: "Don't Monkey with the Score" advertising Sturges & Burn Mfg. Co.
Four American floral celluloid trump indicators with floral decorations

American celluloid trump indicator for William V. Graeser Furrier

Five celluloid scorers featuring advertisements

Six paper parrot trump indicators
American celluloid score card for Portable Machinery Company

Celluloid paper trump indicator for Peter Doelger bottled beer
Two English Charles Goodall & Sons whist scorers
Three paper trump indicators featuring advertisements
Paper trump indicator for De la Rue
Paper trump indicator for Goodalls
Paper trump indicator for Moore Stock Co.
Two Progressive Pedro score cards featuring advertisements for Victor & Co.
Four small circular whist scorers in cardboard decorative box with text "Marken Zum Anlegen"
American tin card marker featuring advertisement for Zonweiss
Posters and artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains artwork depicting playing card imagery and advertising posters for playing card manufacturers. Material includes lithographs, etchings, and paintings.

did.unittitle: Two Henry Heath lithographs: Heath's Court Beauties No. 3 and 5
Framed etching featuring children building a card house
Four etchings featuring children building card houses
Two French advertisement posters for Grimaud featuring a Queen and a King
American poster advertising Kinney Bros. cigarette brands and featuring a woman with Kinney Harlequin playing cards
American Russell & Morgan Printing Co. poster for United States Playing Cards featuring a tiger 12" x 22"
American Russell & Morgan Printing Co. poster for United States Playing Cards featuring a tiger 12" x 14"
Four Art Nouveau lithographed French menu sheets designed by Francisco Tamagno
Painting of seven English playing cards
Fossorier & Amar French lithograph poster featuring a queen of spades by artist Henri Bellery Desfontaines and printed by Draeger
Four Congress Playing Card posters
The Playing Card Museum poster for the United States Playing Card Co.
The Playing Card Museum poster for the United States Playing Card Co.
The Playing Card Museum poster for the United States Playing Card Co.
The Playing Card Museum poster for the United States Playing Card Co.
American cigar box advertisement poster for Trump cigars
Five British court card illustrations
Exposition Universelle Grand Prix poster reproduction
French painting depicting the building of a card house
New York Consolidated Card Company poster by F. Heppenheimer's Sons, New York
Instruction and rule booklets and publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes printed material related to playing cards and other games. Included are booklets, catalogs, magazines, and publications. Material originates from England, France, and the United States

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of printed material.

title.part: American Playing Card Co. Book of Card Rules

did.unittitle: The Art Journal vol. VII
The Bath Club Rules for "Cooncan" 4th edition
Bestway Series No. 185

Deutsche Spielkaten by Otto Reisig
Entertaining with Cards 2nd, 3rd, and 4th editions
Explanatory Notes of a Pack of Cavalier Playing Cards. Temp. Charles II. Forming a Complete Political Satire of the Commonwealth by Edmund Goldsmid
8 game instruction pamphlets: two Euchre, Pinochle, Fortune Telling, Fan Tan, Hearts, Whist, and Skat
Golfer and Sportsman Magazine

Harpers Weekly featuring "A Valentine Romance of ye Olden Times"

Magazines featuring playing card imagery on the cover

did.unittitle: Whist and Duplicate Whist revised and corrected by Walter H. Barney
Whist and Duplicate Whist revised and corrected by Walter H. Barney
Objects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes objects depicting playing cards and other games. Present in the series are bobeches, card boxes and cases, dishes, spoons, textiles, trump indicators, and other material. Material originates from Canada, England, France, Germany, Mexico, and the United States.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Objects are arranged chronologically. Other material is further arranged by material type.

did.unittitle: 16 mother of pearl gaming counters
Two French blue painted glass bobeches featuring an eight card motif
Textile with two figures playing a card game and the text "cribbage"
American celluloid penknife edged in silver with four pips set into handle
Various whist and bridge game pins, buttons, and pendants
Annual dinner menu on 15 fashion series cards for United States Playing Card Co. employees, housed in an imitation book box
Display case with glass front advertising English company De la Rue & Goodall
Tin display stand for twelve playing card decks
Andrew Dougherty fabric satchel
Dawson's brewery plastic beer foam scraper
Decorative cardboard box with playing card imagery
Lidded box with mother of pearl domino inlay
Queen of hearts and gold detail decorative object
Ceramic mugs, plates, card holders, and other ceramic objects
English porcelain mug with playing card designs
Four Italian plates featuring clowns and animal entertainers and "Pagliacci, Soc. Cer. Richard" mark on back
French porcelain tea bag holder featuring playing cards
Four porcelain whist scorers featuring suited kings and scoring words
English ashtray advertising Schwepps beverages and commemorating 60 years of the Queen's reign
German hanging match holder featuring the diamond jack
German Shakespeare porcelain cigarette holder featuring playing cards and King Henry VIII quote
German Shakespeare porcelain salt and pepper shakers featuring playing cards and King Henry VIII quote
German porcelain cup and saucer outlined in gold featuring English cards
German porcelain cup and saucer outlined in gold featuring German cards
English coffee cup featuring court figures
Ceramic German king of clubs card holder with tarot decks
Ceramic German queen of hearts card holder with tarot decks
Ceramic German lidded king of clubs card holder
Ceramic German lidded queen of hearts card holder
English cup and saucer marked "Silicon China Ceylon Ivory" with diamond jack and suit signs
Four German table markers featuring elephants standing on the four different suit signs
German pin tray featuring a hand holding three cards
United States Playing Card Co. ceramic dish
Spoons, card holders, ashtrays, coins, and other silver and metal objects
English Battersea enamel over copper reticulated basket
Sterling silver spoon with image of Providence R.I. in bowl
Sterling silver spoon with spade ace and "Whist 1st Prize" engraved in bowl
Sterling silver spoon with winning Euchre hand on handle and "Euchre" in bowl
Sterling silver stick pin with hand holding four aces at the top
Sterling spoon featuring an engraved image and slogan in the bowl and a hand holding cards
Sterling spoon with four cards spread in hand on top of handle and gilt wash in bowl
American silver matchsafe featuring a spade, ace, and scorers
Two American miniature sterling silver card holders with colorful enameled card spreads on front
English double sterling silver card box featuring an oval enameled picture of a white dog with two patience decks
Sterling silver spoon with enameled aces in the bowl
Sterling silver spoon with suit signs and the word "Bridge" on handle with picture of man "going back" in bowl
Sterling silver spoon with "Whist Elm Tree Inn 1900" in the bowl
Mexican double silver card box with silver plate holder to separate two decks, featuring an enameled suit sign and floral engravings
Sterling spoon with enameled suit signs down the handle and a picture of the "Mining Building A.Y.P. Expo Seattle 1909"
American sterling silver card box with cutout club on one side and spade on other, marked 397 on bottom
Round locket featuring four aces and an Art Nouveau woman engraved on front
Sterling silver picture frame with pink satin background and cross mark on bottom
Sterling teaspoon with enameled suit signs on handle
Canadian sterling teaspoon with the four suit signs on the top of handle
American sterling spoon with handle in the shape of king holding a club and sword and suit sign cutouts in bowl
American sterling spoon with handle in the shape of queen holding a club and suit sign cutouts in bowl
French sterling silver ashtray with pips on each corner
Silver card case featuring Deco cutwork filigree designs incorporating suit signs and leaves
Silver-plate two-sided trump indicator featuring four enameled suit signs and three celluloid sheets inside to write scores on
American sterling spoon with suit sign cutouts along the handle
Sterling silver trophy with enameled suits signs entwined on crest, Whitehead Bridge Studios
Sterling silver spoon commemorating the birth of Carole Ann Werner
Ashtray by bridge headquarters official featuring an ace and "OLTC Olympic"
Card indicator with turning suits
Two Dawson's Brewery bottle openers
Decorative brass playing card press
Enamel cup featuring advertisements for Dickinson's
German metal trump indicator with rotating suits
Suze French advertising tin featuring a Judith playing card
King Kard container for Sam Robinson clothing and shoes
85 playing card coins
Playing card folder featuring playing card imagery
Silver and wood box with open cut out of an ace
Sterling silver spoon with "Euchre" in the bowl and handle with flowers and ribbon
Sterling silver spoon with leaf and cards on handle
Sterling silver spoon with hand holding enameled cards on handle
Gilded spoon with hand holding cards in bowl
Card holder with enamel images of a king and queen
Computer media

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
